name: Afghanistan
fact: The Buddhas of Bamiyan were two giant cliff carvings—among the largest in the world before their destruction in 2001.

name: Åland Islands
fact: Åland is an autonomous, Swedish-speaking archipelago that belongs to Finland but has its own parliament and flag.

name: Albania
fact: During the Cold War, Albania built hundreds of thousands of concrete bunkers—more per person than anywhere else on Earth.

name: Algeria
fact: About 90% of Algeria is covered by the Sahara, yet most of its people live along the Mediterranean coast.

name: American Samoa
fact: American Samoa is the only U.S. territory south of the equator and is famous for its NFL players and tuna industry.

name: Andorra
fact: Andorra has no airport or railway—visitors arrive by road through mountain passes from France or Spain.

name: Angola
fact: Angola's Kalandula Falls are among the largest and most powerful waterfalls in Africa.

name: Anguilla
fact: Anguilla's beaches are so prized that the island has repeatedly voted against full independence to keep ties with Britain.

name: Antarctica
fact: Antarctica holds about 90% of the world's ice and roughly 70% of its fresh water.

name: Antigua and Barbuda
fact: Antigua claims 365 beaches—one for every day of the year.

name: Argentina
fact: Argentina's Perito Moreno Glacier is one of the few major glaciers in the world that is still advancing.

name: Armenia
fact: Mount Ararat, visible from much of Armenia, is a national symbol though it lies just across the border in Türkiye.

name: Aruba
fact: Aruba sits outside the Caribbean hurricane belt, giving it reliably sunny weather year-round.

name: Australia
fact: Australia is home to more venomous snakes than any other country—and most of its wildlife is found nowhere else.

name: Austria
fact: Vienna ranked among Europe's great imperial capitals; Mozart, Beethoven, and Strauss all made their mark here.

name: Azerbaijan
fact: Nearly half of Azerbaijan lies below sea level thanks to the Caspian shoreline and the low-lying Kura plain.

name: Bahamas
fact: The Bahamas has more than 700 islands and cays spread across clear turquoise shallows.

name: Bahrain
fact: Bahrain's Tree of Life is a lone mesquite tree thriving in the desert with no obvious water source for centuries.

name: Bangladesh
fact: Bangladesh crams more than 170 million people into a delta the size of Iowa—one of the densest countries on Earth.

name: Barbados
fact: Barbados is the easternmost island of the Caribbean and was once a major center of British sugar production.

name: Belarus
fact: About 40% of Belarus is forested, earning it the nickname 'the lungs of Europe.'

name: Belgium
fact: Belgium produces more than 1,500 varieties of beer and has been brewing for centuries.

name: Belize
fact: Belize is home to the Great Blue Hole, a perfect circular sinkhole visible from space.

name: Benin
fact: Benin was once the powerful Kingdom of Dahomey, whose all-female warrior units inspired the Dora Milaje in Black Panther.

name: Bermuda
fact: Bermuda's pink-sand beaches get their color from crushed coral and shell fragments mixed with white sand.

name: Bhutan
fact: Bhutan measures progress with Gross National Happiness instead of focusing only on economic output.

name: Bolivia
fact: Bolivia has two capital cities: La Paz is the administrative seat and Sucre the constitutional capital.

name: Bosnia and Herzegovina
fact: Mostar's Ottoman-era Stari Most bridge was destroyed in 1993 and painstakingly rebuilt stone by stone.

name: Botswana
fact: Botswana's Okavango Delta is one of the world's largest inland deltas—a desert oasis teeming with wildlife.

name: Bouvet Island
fact: Bouvet Island is one of the most remote uninhabited islands on Earth, more than 1,000 miles from Antarctica.

name: Brazil
fact: Brazil contains about 60% of the Amazon rainforest—the largest tropical forest on the planet.

name: British Indian Ocean Territory
fact: The Chagos Archipelago includes Diego Garcia, a strategically vital U.S.–U.K. military base in the Indian Ocean.

name: British Virgin Islands
fact: The British Virgin Islands are a sailing paradise with dozens of sheltered bays and reef-protected waters.

name: Brunei
officialName: Nation of Brunei, Abode of Peace
aliases: nation of brunei, abode of peace
fact: Brunei is one of the world's richest nations per capita, built largely on offshore oil and gas.

name: Bulgaria
fact: Bulgaria is one of the world's oldest countries and still uses the Cyrillic alphabet invented by local saints.

name: Burkina Faso
fact: Burkina Faso means 'land of upright people' in Mossi and Dioula.

name: Burundi
fact: Burundi and Rwanda were once a single kingdom before European colonization split them.

name: Cambodia
fact: Angkor Wat in Cambodia is the largest religious monument in the world by land area.

name: Cameroon
fact: Cameroon is nicknamed 'Africa in miniature' for its coast, rainforest, savanna, and volcanic highlands in one country.

name: Canada
fact: Canada has more lakes than the rest of the world's countries combined.

name: Cape Verde
fact: Cape Verde's music—especially morna—has influenced styles across the Atlantic world.

name: Caribbean Netherlands
fact: Bonaire, Sint Eustatius, and Saba are Caribbean municipalities of the Netherlands known for diving and reefs.

name: Cayman Islands
fact: The Cayman Islands are a global financial hub with more registered companies than residents.

name: Central African Republic
fact: The Central African Republic sits near the continent's geographic center, largely covered in rainforest.

name: Chad
fact: Lake Chad, which Chad is named for, has shrunk dramatically since the 1960s—an environmental crisis for the region.

name: Chile
fact: Chile is more than 4,000 km long but averages only about 177 km wide—one of the world's most stretched countries.

name: China
fact: China's Great Wall isn't one continuous wall—it's a network of fortifications built over many centuries.

name: Christmas Island
fact: Christmas Island is famous for the annual red crab migration, when millions of crabs cross roads and beaches.

name: Cocos (Keeling) Islands
fact: The Cocos (Keeling) Islands are a remote coral atoll group in the Indian Ocean with a population under 600.

name: Colombia
fact: Colombia produces more emeralds than any other country and is one of the world's top coffee exporters.

name: Comoros
fact: Comoros is one of the world's largest producers of ylang-ylang, a flower essential to many perfumes.

name: Congo
fact: The Congo Basin rainforest is the world's second-largest tropical forest after the Amazon.

name: Cook Islands
fact: Cook Islanders carry New Zealand passports but govern themselves in free association with Wellington.

name: Costa Rica
fact: Costa Rica abolished its army in 1948 and redirected military spending to education and health care.

name: Croatia
fact: Dubrovnik's medieval walls in Croatia became a filming location for King's Landing in Game of Thrones.

name: Cuba
fact: Cuba's classic 1950s American cars are still on the road thanks to decades of ingenious local repair.

name: Curaçao
fact: Curaçao's Handelskade waterfront is lined with pastel Dutch colonial buildings overlooking the harbor.

name: Cyprus
fact: Cyprus is said to be the birthplace of Aphrodite, the Greek goddess of love.

name: Czechia
fact: Prague's Astronomical Clock in Czechia has been keeping time since 1410—one of the oldest still working.

name: Denmark
fact: Denmark's LEGO bricks were invented in Billund, where the original LEGOLAND theme park still stands.

name: Djibouti
fact: Djibouti sits on the Afar Triple Junction where three tectonic plates pull apart—creating dramatic rift landscapes.

name: Dominica
fact: Dominica's Boiling Lake is the world's second-largest hot spring and steams like a giant cauldron.

name: Dominican Republic
fact: The Dominican Republic shares Hispaniola with Haiti—the only island split between two nations in the Caribbean.

name: DR Congo
fact: The Congo River is the world's deepest river and second-largest by discharge, cutting through DR Congo's rainforests.

name: Ecuador
fact: Ecuador is named for the equator, which runs through the country—and the Galápagos Islands belong to it.

name: Egypt
fact: The Great Pyramid of Giza was the tallest human-made structure in the world for nearly 4,000 years.

name: El Salvador
fact: El Salvador is the smallest and most densely populated country in Central America.

name: Equatorial Guinea
fact: Equatorial Guinea is the only African country with Spanish as an official language.

name: Eritrea
fact: Eritrea's capital Asmara has one of the world's finest collections of Art Deco and Modernist architecture.

name: Estonia
fact: Estonia is one of the world's most digital societies—citizens can vote, sign documents, and file taxes online.

name: Eswatini
fact: Eswatini (formerly Swaziland) is one of the world's last absolute monarchies in Africa.

name: Ethiopia
fact: Ethiopia follows its own calendar—about seven to eight years behind the Gregorian calendar most of the world uses.

name: Falkland Islands
fact: The Falkland Islands were the setting of a brief but intense war between Britain and Argentina in 1982.

name: Faroe Islands
fact: Faroe Islanders drive through tunnels between islands and generate much of their power from wind and hydro.

name: Fiji
fact: Fiji straddles the International Date Line, so it is among the first countries to greet each new day.

name: Finland
fact: Finland has more saunas than cars—roughly one sauna for every two people.

name: France
fact: France is the most visited country on Earth, drawing more international tourists than any other nation.

name: French Guiana
fact: French Guiana hosts Europe's main spaceport at Kourou—rockets launch from South America but under French flag.

name: French Polynesia
fact: French Polynesia's Bora Bora and Tahiti helped define the world's image of a tropical paradise.

name: French Southern and Antarctic Lands
officialName: Territory of the French Southern and Antarctic Lands
aliases: territory of the french southern and antarctic lands
fact: The French Southern Territories include remote sub-Antarctic islands with no permanent human population.

name: Gabon
fact: Gabon preserves about 11% of its land in national parks—among the highest proportions in the world.

name: Gambia
fact: The Gambia is a narrow strip of land along the Gambia River, surrounded on three sides by Senegal.

name: Georgia
fact: Georgia claims to be the birthplace of wine—archaeologists found 8,000-year-old winemaking evidence here.

name: Germany
fact: Germany's Autobahn famously has long stretches with no official speed limit for cars.

name: Ghana
fact: Ghana was the first sub-Saharan African country to gain independence from colonial rule, in 1957.

name: Gibraltar
fact: The Rock of Gibraltar is a limestone promontory guarded by wild Barbary macaques—the only wild monkeys in Europe.

name: Greece
fact: Greece has thousands of islands, though only a few hundred are inhabited.

name: Greenland
fact: Greenland is the world's largest island and is covered by an ice sheet up to three kilometers thick.

name: Grenada
fact: Grenada is known as the 'Spice Isle' for its nutmeg, mace, and cinnamon exports.

name: Guadeloupe
fact: Guadeloupe's La Soufrière volcano last erupted in 1976, forcing the evacuation of 73,000 people.

name: Guam
fact: Guam is the westernmost U.S. territory and a key military hub in the Pacific.

name: Guatemala
fact: Guatemala's Tikal ruins were a major Maya city—its temples rise above the Petén jungle canopy.

name: Guernsey
fact: Guernsey cows, famous worldwide for rich milk, take their name from this Channel Island.

name: Guinea
fact: Guinea holds about one-third of the world's bauxite—the ore used to make aluminum.

name: Guinea-Bissau
fact: Guinea-Bissau's Bijagós Archipelago is a UNESCO biosphere reserve of mangroves and rare sea turtles.

name: Guyana
fact: About 80% of Guyana is covered by pristine Amazon rainforest—one of the highest percentages in South America.

name: Haiti
fact: Haiti was the first nation founded by formerly enslaved people who won independence, in 1804.

name: Heard Island and McDonald Islands
fact: Heard Island and McDonald Islands are among the most remote places on Earth, near Antarctica.

name: Honduras
fact: Honduras's Copán ruins feature some of the finest Maya stone carving in Central America.

name: Hong Kong
officialName: Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of the People's Republic of China
aliases: hong kong special administrative region of the people's republic of china
fact: Hong Kong has more skyscrapers than any other city in the world—well over 500 buildings above 150 meters.

name: Hungary
fact: Hungary's capital Budapest was formed by merging three cities and sits atop more than 100 thermal springs.

name: Iceland
fact: Iceland runs almost entirely on renewable energy and sits atop the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, splitting two continents.

name: India
fact: India has 22 official languages and is on track to become the world's most populous country.

name: Indonesia
fact: Indonesia is the world's largest archipelago—more than 17,000 islands spanning three time zones.

name: Iran
fact: Persian carpets from Iran are so prized that fine antique rugs can sell for millions of dollars.

name: Iraq
fact: Mesopotamia—'the land between the rivers'—in modern Iraq is where writing, the wheel, and cities first emerged.

name: Ireland
fact: Ireland is called the Emerald Isle for its lush green countryside shaped by Gulf Stream rains.

name: Isle of Man
fact: The Isle of Man is a self-governing Crown dependency famous for the TT motorcycle races since 1907.

name: Israel
fact: The Dead Sea shoreline in Israel is the lowest dry land on Earth—about 430 meters below sea level.

name: Italy
fact: Italy has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country—58 and counting.

name: Ivory Coast
fact: Ivory Coast produces about 40% of the world's cocoa, making it the top chocolate ingredient supplier.

name: Jamaica
fact: Jamaica gave the world reggae—Bob Marley's music turned a small island sound into a global movement.

name: Japan
fact: Japan has more than 6,800 islands and sits on the Pacific Ring of Fire with over 100 active volcanoes.

name: Jersey
fact: Jersey's tidal range is among the largest in the world—at low tide, the island nearly doubles in size.

name: Jordan
fact: Petra in Jordan is a rose-red city carved into sandstone cliffs by the Nabataeans over 2,000 years ago.

name: Kazakhstan
fact: Kazakhstan is the world's largest landlocked country and was the last Soviet republic to declare independence.

name: Kenya
fact: Kenya's Great Rift Valley lakes draw millions of flamingos, turning shores pink during migration.

name: Kiribati
officialName: Independent and Sovereign Republic of Kiribati
aliases: independent and sovereign republic of kiribati
fact: Kiribati is one of the first countries likely to lose land to rising seas—some islands sit just two meters above water.

name: Kosovo
fact: Kosovo declared independence in 2008; its flag shows a map of the country with six stars for its main ethnic groups.

name: Kuwait
fact: Kuwait's Liberation Tower was named after the 1991 Gulf War that ended the Iraqi occupation.

name: Kyrgyzstan
fact: Kyrgyzstan is over 90% mountainous—the Tian Shan range dominates the landscape.

name: Laos
fact: Laos is the most heavily bombed country per capita in history due to the Secret War during the Vietnam era.

name: Latvia
fact: Riga, Latvia's capital, has one of Europe's largest collections of Art Nouveau architecture.

name: Lebanon
fact: Lebanon's cedars appear on its flag and were prized by ancient Egyptians and Phoenicians for shipbuilding.

name: Lesotho
fact: Lesotho is entirely surrounded by South Africa and is the only country in the world that lies entirely above 1,000 meters.

name: Liberia
fact: Liberia was founded in the 19th century by freed American slaves—its flag echoes the U.S. stars and stripes.

name: Libya
fact: Much of Libya lies within the Sahara, including the ancient Roman ruins of Leptis Magna on the coast.

name: Liechtenstein
fact: Liechtenstein is one of only two doubly landlocked countries—surrounded by landlocked nations.

name: Lithuania
fact: Lithuania was the last European nation to abandon paganism and the first Soviet republic to declare independence.

name: Luxembourg
fact: Luxembourg has the highest GDP per capita in the world and is a founding member of the European Union.

name: Macau
officialName: Macao Special Administrative Region of the People's Republic of China
aliases: macao special administrative region of the people's republic of china
fact: Macau was a Portuguese colony for 400 years before returning to China in 1999—it rivals Las Vegas for gambling revenue.

name: Madagascar
fact: Madagascar broke away from Africa 88 million years ago—about 90% of its wildlife is found nowhere else.

name: Malawi
fact: Lake Malawi contains more species of fish than any other lake on Earth—hundreds of colorful cichlids.

name: Malaysia
fact: Malaysia's Petronas Towers in Kuala Lumpur were the world's tallest buildings from 1998 to 2004.

name: Maldives
fact: The Maldives is the world's lowest country—its highest natural point is only about 2.4 meters above sea level.

name: Mali
fact: Timbuktu in Mali was once a legendary center of Islamic learning and trans-Saharan trade.

name: Malta
fact: Malta's Megalithic Temples are older than Stonehenge and the Egyptian pyramids.

name: Marshall Islands
fact: The Marshall Islands include Bikini Atoll, where U.S. nuclear tests in the 1940s–50s forced entire communities to relocate.

name: Martinique
fact: Martinique's Mount Pelée erupted in 1902, destroying the city of Saint-Pierre in minutes.

name: Mauritania
fact: Mauritania's Richat Structure—a giant bull's-eye in the Sahara—is visible from space and was once thought to be an impact crater.

name: Mauritius
fact: Mauritius was the only known home of the dodo before the flightless bird was hunted to extinction by the 1680s.

name: Mayotte
fact: Mayotte sits in the Comoros archipelago but chose to remain French—a source of ongoing regional tension.

name: Mexico
fact: Mexico City was built on the ruins of Tenochtitlán, the Aztec capital that once floated on a lake.

name: Micronesia
fact: Micronesia spans more than 2.7 million square kilometers of ocean but has a land area smaller than Rhode Island.

name: Moldova
fact: Moldova's Mileștii Mici holds one of the world's largest wine cellars—over 200 km of underground tunnels.

name: Monaco
fact: Monaco is smaller than Central Park in New York yet packs luxury casinos, a Grand Prix, and a royal palace.

name: Mongolia
fact: Genghis Khan's empire began on the Mongolian steppe—the country still celebrates him as a founding father.

name: Montenegro
fact: Montenegro means 'Black Mountain' in Venetian Italian, named for the dark forests on Mount Lovćen.

name: Montserrat
fact: Montserrat's Soufrière Hills volcano erupted in 1995, burying the capital Plymouth in ash and forcing evacuation.

name: Morocco
fact: Morocco's Chefchaouen is famous for its buildings painted in shades of blue.

name: Mozambique
fact: Mozambique's Bazaruto Archipelago protects dugongs—rare sea cows once mistaken for mermaids by sailors.

name: Myanmar
officialName: Republic of the Union of Myanmar
aliases: republic of the union of myanmar
fact: Myanmar's Shwedagon Pagoda in Yangon is covered in gold leaf and topped with thousands of diamonds and rubies.

name: Namibia
fact: Namibia's Namib Desert is considered the world's oldest desert—some dunes are millions of years old.

name: Nauru
fact: Nauru was once among the richest countries per capita thanks to phosphate mining—but the deposits are nearly gone.

name: Nepal
fact: Mount Everest's summit sits on the Nepal–China border—the world's highest point at 8,849 meters.

name: Netherlands
fact: About a quarter of the Netherlands lies below sea level, protected by an elaborate system of dikes and pumps.

name: New Caledonia
fact: New Caledonia has the world's largest lagoon, surrounded by the world's second-longest barrier reef.

name: New Zealand
fact: New Zealand was the first country to give women the right to vote, in 1893.

name: Nicaragua
fact: Nicaragua has the two largest freshwater lakes in Central America—Lake Nicaragua even has freshwater sharks.

name: Niger
fact: The Sahara covers most of Niger, yet the country takes its name from the Niger River that arcs through its southwest.

name: Nigeria
fact: Nigeria has Africa's largest population and Nollywood produces more films than Hollywood by volume.

name: Niue
fact: Niue is one of the world's largest raised coral atolls and uses New Zealand currency.

name: Norfolk Island
fact: Norfolk Island was settled by descendants of the Bounty mutineers after they left Pitcairn.

name: North Korea
fact: North Korea and South Korea are still technically at war—the 1953 armistice never became a peace treaty.

name: North Macedonia
fact: North Macedonia's Lake Ohrid is one of Europe's oldest and deepest lakes, home to species found nowhere else.

name: Northern Mariana Islands
officialName: Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands
aliases: commonwealth of the northern mariana islands
fact: The Mariana Trench near the Northern Mariana Islands is the deepest point in the ocean—almost 11 km down.

name: Norway
fact: Norway's fjords were carved by Ice Age glaciers and its coastline would stretch halfway around the equator if straightened.

name: Oman
fact: Oman's Sultan Qaboos transformed the country from an isolated sultanate into a modern nation over his 50-year reign.

name: Pakistan
fact: Pakistan is home to K2, the world's second-highest mountain—harder to climb than Everest and nicknamed the Savage Mountain.

name: Palau
fact: Palau's Jellyfish Lake lets swimmers snorkel among millions of harmless golden jellyfish.

name: Palestine
fact: Jericho in the Palestinian territories is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world.

name: Panama
fact: The Panama Canal saves ships a 13,000-km journey around South America by cutting through the isthmus.

name: Papua New Guinea
officialName: Independent State of Papua New Guinea
aliases: independent state of papua new guinea
fact: Papua New Guinea has more than 800 living languages—more than any other country.

name: Paraguay
fact: Paraguay's Guaraní language is one of the few indigenous American languages with official status alongside Spanish.

name: Peru
fact: Machu Picchu in Peru is a 15th-century Inca citadel perched 2,430 meters above sea level in the Andes.

name: Philippines
fact: The Philippines consists of about 7,641 islands and is the world's top exporter of nurses.

name: Pitcairn Islands
fact: Pitcairn Island was settled by mutineers from HMS Bounty—today fewer than 50 people live there.

name: Poland
fact: Poland's Wieliczka Salt Mine has chapels, sculptures, and chandeliers carved entirely from salt.

name: Portugal
fact: Portugal's explorers opened sea routes to India, Brazil, and Japan—shaping the first global age of trade.

name: Puerto Rico
fact: Puerto Rico's bioluminescent bays glow blue at night when microscopic organisms are disturbed in the water.

name: Qatar
fact: Qatar will have hosted a FIFA World Cup where every stadium was within about an hour's drive of each other.

name: Réunion
fact: Réunion's Piton de la Fournaise is one of the world's most active volcanoes, erupting dozens of times since 2000.

name: Romania
fact: Romania's Transylvania region inspired Bram Stoker's Dracula—though Vlad the Impaler never lived in Bran Castle.

name: Russia
fact: Russia spans 11 time zones and crosses both Europe and Asia—it is the largest country on Earth by area.

name: Rwanda
fact: Rwanda banned plastic bags nationwide in 2008 and holds a monthly community cleanup called Umuganda.

name: Saint Barthélemy
fact: Saint Barthélemy is a glamorous Caribbean island nicknamed St. Barts, popular with yachts and celebrities.

name: Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha
officialName: Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha
aliases: saint helena, ascension and tristan da cunha
fact: Saint Helena is where Napoleon was exiled after Waterloo—it took ships weeks to reach from Europe.

name: Saint Kitts and Nevis
officialName: Federation of Saint Christopher and Nevis
aliases: federation of saint christopher and nevis
fact: Saint Kitts and Nevis is the smallest sovereign country in the Western Hemisphere by both area and population.

name: Saint Lucia
fact: Saint Lucia's twin Pitons—volcanic spires rising from the sea—are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Saint Martin
fact: Saint Martin is split between France and the Netherlands—the smallest landmass shared by two nations.

name: Saint Pierre and Miquelon
fact: Saint Pierre and Miquelon are French islands off Canada's coast—residents use the euro just 25 km from Newfoundland.

name: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
fact: Saint Vincent's La Soufrière volcano erupted in 2021, covering much of the island in ash.

name: Samoa
fact: Samoa skipped December 30, 2011 entirely when it moved across the International Date Line to align with trading partners.

name: San Marino
officialName: Most Serene Republic of San Marino
aliases: most serene republic of san marino
fact: San Marino claims to be the world's oldest republic, founded in 301 AD on Mount Titano.

name: São Tomé and Príncipe
officialName: Democratic Republic of São Tomé and Príncipe
aliases: democratic republic of são tomé and príncipe
fact: São Tomé and Príncipe's cocoa plantations once made it a major chocolate supplier to Europe.

name: Saudi Arabia
fact: Saudi Arabia has no permanent rivers—the country is mostly desert, including the vast Empty Quarter.

name: Senegal
fact: Senegal's Lake Retba is bright pink from salt-loving algae and is harvested for salt like the Dead Sea.

name: Serbia
fact: Serbia sits at a crossroads of empires—Belgrade is one of Europe's oldest continuously inhabited cities.

name: Seychelles
fact: Seychelles's Vallée de Mai preserves the coco de mer, a palm that produces the world's heaviest seed.

name: Sierra Leone
fact: Sierra Leone's name means 'lion mountains' in Portuguese, though lions no longer live there.

name: Singapore
fact: Singapore transformed from a fishing village to a global financial hub in a single generation.

name: Sint Maarten
fact: Sint Maarten's Maho Beach is famous for airliners passing just meters overhead on approach to the runway.

name: Slovakia
fact: Slovakia has more castles and châteaux per capita than almost any other country in Europe.

name: Slovenia
fact: Slovenia's Postojna Cave holds a unique species of blind salamander called the olm—or 'human fish.'

name: Solomon Islands
fact: Solomon Islanders fought alongside U.S. Marines in World War II's brutal Guadalcanal campaign.

name: Somalia
fact: Somalia has the longest coastline on mainland Africa but has struggled with piracy off its shores.

name: South Africa
fact: South Africa has three capital cities—each hosts a different branch of government.

name: South Georgia
officialName: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
aliases: south georgia and the south sandwich islands
fact: South Georgia's Grytviken whaling station is where explorer Ernest Shackleton is buried.

name: South Korea
fact: South Korea's Hangul alphabet was scientifically designed in 1443 so anyone could learn to read.

name: South Sudan
fact: South Sudan is the world's newest widely recognized country, gaining independence from Sudan in 2011.

name: Spain
fact: Spain's Sagrada Família in Barcelona has been under construction since 1882 and is still not finished.

name: Sri Lanka
officialName: Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
aliases: democratic socialist republic of sri lanka
fact: Sri Lanka was once called Ceylon and produces Ceylon tea from highland plantations.

name: Sudan
fact: Sudan has more pyramids than Egypt—ancient Nubian kingdoms left hundreds along the Nile.

name: Suriname
fact: Suriname is the smallest independent country in South America and one of the most forested.

name: Svalbard and Jan Mayen
fact: Svalbard's Global Seed Vault stores backup copies of crop seeds from around the world in Arctic permafrost.

name: Sweden
fact: Sweden's Nobel Prizes are awarded each year in Stockholm—except Peace, which is given in Oslo.

name: Switzerland
fact: Switzerland has four official languages and has not fought a foreign war since 1815.

name: Syria
fact: Damascus in Syria is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world.

name: Taiwan
fact: Taiwan's Taipei 101 was the world's tallest building from 2004 to 2010 and is designed to withstand typhoons and earthquakes.

name: Tajikistan
fact: Tajikistan is over 90% mountainous—the Pamir range is nicknamed the 'Roof of the World.'

name: Tanzania
fact: Tanzania's Serengeti hosts the largest terrestrial mammal migration on Earth—over a million wildebeest.

name: Thailand
fact: Thailand is the only Southeast Asian country never colonized by a European power.

name: Timor-Leste
fact: Timor-Leste is one of the world's youngest countries, gaining full independence in 2002 after centuries of occupation.

name: Togo
fact: Togo is one of the world's top producers of phosphates, used in fertilizer.

name: Tokelau
fact: Tokelau is one of the first territories to produce more than 100% of its electricity from solar power.

name: Tonga
fact: Tonga is the only Pacific nation that was never fully colonized—it became a constitutional monarchy in 1875.

name: Trinidad and Tobago
fact: Trinidad and Tobago invented the steelpan—the only acoustic instrument invented in the 20th century.

name: Tunisia
fact: Tunisia's ancient city of Carthage once rivaled Rome before being destroyed and rebuilt by the Romans.

name: Türkiye
fact: Istanbul in Türkiye is the only major city spanning two continents—Europe and Asia.

name: Turkmenistan
fact: Turkmenistan's Darvaza gas crater has burned continuously in the desert since Soviet engineers lit it in 1971.

name: Turks and Caicos Islands
fact: Turks and Caicos has some of the clearest turquoise water in the Caribbean and a thriving conch fishery.

name: Tuvalu
fact: Tuvalu is among the world's smallest and most low-lying countries—rising seas threaten its very existence.

name: Uganda
fact: Uganda is one of the few places where you can see mountain gorillas in the wild—about half the world's population lives here.

name: Ukraine
fact: Ukraine is one of the world's largest grain exporters—the 'breadbasket of Europe.'

name: United Arab Emirates
fact: Dubai in the UAE built the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa, at 828 meters.

name: United Kingdom
officialName: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
aliases: united kingdom of great britain and northern ireland
fact: The United Kingdom is made up of four countries—England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.

name: United States
fact: The United States has the world's largest economy and is the third most populous country.

name: United States Minor Outlying Islands
fact: The U.S. Minor Outlying Islands include Midway Atoll, site of a pivotal 1942 naval battle in World War II.

name: United States Virgin Islands
officialName: Virgin Islands of the United States
aliases: virgin islands of the united states
fact: The U.S. Virgin Islands were purchased from Denmark in 1917 for $25 million in gold.

name: Uruguay
fact: Uruguay was the first country to legalize recreational cannabis nationwide, in 2013.

name: Uzbekistan
fact: Uzbekistan's Registan in Samarkand was a glittering center of the Silk Road trade route.

name: Vanuatu
fact: Vanuatu invented bungee jumping—local land divers on Pentecost Island inspired the modern sport.

name: Vatican City
fact: Vatican City is the world's smallest independent state—about 44 hectares enclosed within Rome.

name: Venezuela
fact: Venezuela's Angel Falls is the world's highest uninterrupted waterfall, plunging 979 meters.

name: Vietnam
fact: Vietnam's Ha Long Bay has thousands of limestone karsts rising from emerald water—a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Wallis and Futuna
officialName: Territory of the Wallis and Futuna Islands
aliases: territory of the wallis and futuna islands
fact: Wallis and Futuna is one of the last French territories where traditional kings still hold official authority.

name: Western Sahara
fact: Western Sahara is one of the most sparsely populated places on Earth—mostly desert with a disputed political status.

name: Yemen
fact: Yemen's Old City of Sana'a has distinctive multi-story tower houses decorated with white gypsum patterns.

name: Zambia
fact: Zambia's Victoria Falls—locally called Mosi-oa-Tunya, 'the smoke that thunders'—is one of the world's largest waterfalls.

name: Zimbabwe
fact: Zimbabwe's Great Zimbabwe ruins were the capital of a medieval trading empire that exported gold to the Indian Ocean.
